LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MAKES A DISCLOSURE TO THE PARTIES REGARDING ITS FAMILIARITY WITH ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY FROM SERVING AS A SETTLEMENT JUDGE IN A SEPARATE CASE THAT INVOLVES ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY. THE COURT STATES THAT IT DOES NOT BELIEVE THIS PRECLUDES THE COURT FROM BEING FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AS TO ALL PLAINTIFFS AND TO STAY THIS CASE PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED.(SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) FORMAL ORDER SIGNED. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings As To Plaintiffs Dalessandro, Louria, Byars, Braverman And Corson; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL BRACAMONTES, ESQ. of Bracamontes & Vlasak (Tel: 415-835-6777) appearing for plaintiffs;
Atty. JENNIFER J. WALT, ESQ. of Littler Mendelson (Tel: 415-677-3137) appearing for defendant Stephens Institute d/b/a Academy of Art University.

The Court makes a disclosure to the parties regarding its familiarity with Academy of Art University from serving as a settlement judge in a separate case that involves Academy of Art University. The Court states that it does not believe this precludes the Court from being fair and impartial in this case.

The matter is argued and submitted by counsel for parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Stephens Institute's motion to compel arbitration as to all plaintiffs and to stay this case pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings is granted. Because plaintiffs' claims relate to their employment by AAU, the expiration of the employment agreements do not render the arbitration agreements inapplicable. Per federal and California case law, because the parties' arbitration agreements clearly and unmistakably delegate the threshold question of arbitrability to the arbitrator, all issues regarding arbitrability must be decided by the arbitrator. Plaintiffs do not advance any argument that the delegation clauses, as distinguished from the arbitration language, are unconscionable or otherwise unenforceable. Plaintiffs' arguments regarding the unconscionability of the arbitration agreements, including any arguments that any of the plaintiffs opted out or were deterred from opting out of those agreements, must be heard and decided by the arbitrator.

FORMAL ORDER SIGNED.
